title: Medroxyprogesterone Injection: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text:

What should I do if I forget a dose? 
What side effects can this medication cause? 
What other information should I know? 
Brand names 
Brand names of combination products 
Other names 
IMPORTANT WARNING: 
Women who use medroxyprogesterone injection may lose more bone mineral density which may increase the risk for osteoporosis and bone fractures. This loss in bone mineral density may not be reversible. It is unknown if early use of medroxyprogesterone injection in adolescence or early adulthood will lead to increase risk of osteoporosis and bone fractures later in life.
Tell your doctor if you or anyone in your family has osteoporosis; if you have or have ever had any other bone disease or anorexia nervosa (an eating disorder); or if you drink a lot of alcohol or smoke a great deal. Tell your doctor if you take any of the following medications: corticosteroids such as dexamethasone, methylprednisolone, and prednisone; or medications for seizures such as carbamazepine, phenytoin, or phenobarbital.
You should not use medroxyprogesterone injection for a long time (e.g., more than 2 years) unless no other method of birth control is right for you or no other medication will work to treat your condition. Your doctor may test your bones to be sure they are not becoming too thin before you continue to use medroxyprogesterone injection
